items.title: Tanh vs sigmoid output range
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  inputNode["pre-activation z"]
  tanhNode["tanh(z) in (-1, 1)"]
  sigNode["sigmoid(z) in (0, 1)"]
  zeroNode["tanh(0) = 0 zero-centered"]
  halfNode["sigmoid(0) = 0.5"]
  inputNode --> tanhNode
  inputNode --> sigNode
  tanhNode --> zeroNode
  sigNode --> halfNode
items.caption: Same input, different ranges and center points — the core tanh vs sigmoid distinction owned by this topic.

items.title: Where tanh fits in an RNN step
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  prevNode["h_{t-1}"]
  inputNode["x_t"]
  linearNode["W_h h + W_x x + b"]
  tanhNode["tanh"]
  nextNode["h_t bounded state"]
  prevNode --> linearNode
  inputNode --> linearNode
  linearNode --> tanhNode
  tanhNode --> nextNode
items.caption: Vanilla RNNs apply tanh after the affine combine to produce a zero-centered, bounded hidden state.

items.title: Choosing an activation (high level)
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  startNode["layer role?"]
  probNode["probability in (0,1)?"]
  hiddenNode["bounded hidden / RNN state?"]
  deepNode["deep feedforward hidden?"]
  sigNode["sigmoid"]
  tanhNode["tanh"]
  reluNode["ReLU family"]
  startNode --> probNode
  probNode -->|yes| sigNode
  probNode -->|no| hiddenNode
  hiddenNode -->|yes| tanhNode
  hiddenNode -->|no| deepNode
  deepNode --> reluNode
items.caption: Tanh for zero-centered bounded states; sigmoid for [0,1] outputs/gates; ReLU for typical deep hidden layers.
